message:
refactor(gmail): align capabilities with discovery, add structured send,
scope-limited operation detection, and approval modes

Major capability and safety alignment for the Gmail connector:

Capability ID normalization:
- gmail.messages.read → gmail.read (list, get, thread, labels, sync)
- gmail.messages.send → gmail.send (send_message, send_draft)
- gmail.messages.modify → gmail.write (modify_message, get_draft)
- gmail.labels.manage → gmail.read (list_labels)
- gmail.threads.read → gmail.read (get_thread)
- Add gmail.delete capability for trash_message

Approval modes:
- send_message/send_draft: ApprovalMode::Interactive (Dangerous tier)
- modify_message/trash_message/get_draft: ApprovalMode::Policy (Risky)
- Read operations: None

Structured send_message:
- Accept either raw base64url MIME payload or structured to/subject/body
  fields via anyOf schema
- Add build_raw_message_from_fields() to construct RFC 2822 from fields
- base64 dependency added for URL_SAFE_NO_PAD encoding

Scope-limited operation detection:
- Add granted_scopes_are_authoritative() and
  missing_scope_limited_operations() helpers
- Health endpoint reports scope_limited_operations array
- Doctor adds operation_scope_coverage check
- Self-check degrades to scope_limited when operations are uncovered

Capability verification optimization:
- Replace runtime introspection lookup with static
  required_capability_for_operation() table

client.rs:
- Map 403 Forbidden to GmailError::Unauthorized (Google APIs return 403
  for insufficient scopes, not just 401)

integration.rs:
- Update test assertions for new capability IDs and operation count
- Add approval mode verification helpers

gmail_compliance_e2e.rs:
- Update capability references to gmail.read/gmail.send/gmail.write

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
